summary: Recent updates to Firefox's WebRTC (the technology behind video calls) have improved logging processes to enhance the debugging and performance monitoring capabilities for developers using this framework. Additionally, the expiration of WebRTC DTLS (Datagram Transport Layer Security) keys has been adjusted to align with Firefox version 158, which helps maintain security standards and keep users' communications safe. These changes ensure that developers can better understand how WebRTC is functioning while also reinforcing the security of real-time communications.
